# Setting Up Visual Studio Code

Visual Studio Code (VS Code) is a free, lightweight code editor we 
will use throughout this course to write and run oTree code. Follow 
the instructions below for your operating system.

## macOS

### Installation

1. Go to [https://code.visualstudio.com/](https://code.visualstudio.com/).
2. Click **Download for Mac**.
3. Once downloaded, open the `.zip` file — this extracts **Visual Studio Code.app**.
4. Drag **Visual Studio Code.app** into your **Applications** folder.
5. Open VS Code from your Applications folder or Spotlight Search 
(`Cmd + Space`, type "VS Code").

::: {.callout-tip}
To keep VS Code easily accessible, right-click its icon in the Dock 
and select **Options → Keep in Dock**.
:::



## Windows

### Installation

1. Go to [https://code.visualstudio.com/](https://code.visualstudio.com/).
2. Click **Download for Windows**.
3. Run the downloaded `.exe` installer.
4. Follow the setup wizard and click **Install**, then **Finish**.
5. Launch VS Code from the Start Menu or Desktop shortcut.



## Installing Extensions

Extensions add functionality to VS Code. For this course, you will 
need the following:

### How to Install an Extension

1. Open VS Code.
2. Click the **Extensions** icon in the left sidebar (or press 
`Ctrl+Shift+X` on Windows / `Cmd+Shift+X` on Mac).
3. Type the extension name in the search bar.
4. Click **Install**.

### Required Extensions

| Extension | Publisher | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Python | Microsoft | Core Python language support |
| Pylance | Microsoft | Improved Python code suggestions and error detection |
| Python Debugger | Microsoft | Step-through debugging for your oTree code |

### Recommended Extensions

| Extension | Publisher | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Django | Baptiste Darthenay | Syntax highlighting for oTree HTML templates, which are Django-based |
| Python Environments | Microsoft | Manage Python virtual environments for your oTree projects |
| vscode-icons | VSCode Icons Team | Adds file and folder icons for easier project navigation |

::: {.callout-note}
If you already have VS Code installed, open the Extensions panel and 
check which of these you already have. You only need to install the 
missing ones.
:::
